clinical_notes.summary: 67yo male with COPD GOLD stage 3 (FEV1 38% predicted) and 40 pack-year smoking history (quit 5 years ago) presenting with 3-day worsening dyspnea, increased purulent yellow-green sputum, and rescue inhaler use escalating to 8-10x daily without relief. Baseline mMRC grade 2 dyspnea now present at rest. Denies fever or chest pain. SpO2 88% on room air. Vitals: HR 102, RR 26, BP 142/88. Exam: accessory muscle use, prolonged expiratory phase, diffuse expiratory wheezes, diminished breath sounds at bases. Aspirin allergy (bronchospasm).

chest_xray.summary: Bilateral pulmonary hyperinflation with flattened diaphragms and increased AP diameter consistent with severe COPD. Hyperlucent lung fields with vascular attenuation. No acute focal consolidation. Mild peribronchial wall thickening. Cardiothoracic ratio within normal limits but heart appears elongated (vertical heart). No pneumothorax or pleural effusion.

ecg.summary: Sinus tachycardia at 102 bpm. Right axis deviation (+110 degrees). P pulmonale — peaked P waves in lead II suggestive of right atrial enlargement. QRS 90ms. No acute ST changes. Clockwise rotation with S waves in V5-V6 consistent with right ventricular strain. Low voltage in limb leads. No complete RBBB. Pattern consistent with chronic cor pulmonale from COPD.

echo.summary: Normal LVEF 58%. Right ventricular dilation with mildly reduced RV systolic function. Estimated RVSP 42 mmHg (mildly elevated, consistent with pulmonary hypertension). Flattened interventricular septum (D-sign) during systole consistent with right heart pressure overload. Mild tricuspid regurgitation. Left heart and valves otherwise normal. No pericardial effusion.

heart_sounds.summary: Heart sounds distant due to hyperinflation. Tachycardic rate at 102 bpm. Regular rhythm. Loud P2 component of S2 consistent with elevated pulmonary pressure. No murmurs or gallops. Prominent expiratory wheeze audible without stethoscope. No S3 or S4 appreciated.

lab_results.summary: ABG on room air: pH 7.34, pCO2 52 mmHg (elevated, type 2 respiratory failure), pO2 54 mmHg (hypoxemia), HCO3 28 mEq/L (compensated). WBC 11.8 x10³/µL (mildly elevated). CRP 64 mg/L (elevated). Procalcitonin 0.4 ng/mL (borderline). Hemoglobin 16.2 g/dL (secondary polycythemia). Creatinine 1.0 mg/dL. BNP 220 pg/mL (mildly elevated).

medication.summary: Active medications: Tiotropium 18mcg inhaler daily (LAMA), Fluticasone/Salmeterol 250/50mcg inhaler BID (ICS/LABA), Albuterol inhaler PRN (SABA rescue), Acetaminophen 500mg PRN (osteoarthritis), Omeprazole 20mg daily (GI protection). Aspirin allergy documented (bronchospasm). Initiated: Prednisolone 40mg daily x5 days (COPD exacerbation), Doxycycline 100mg BID x7 days (antibiotic for purulent exacerbation). Supplemental oxygen initiated targeting SpO2 88-92%.
